title: EVA Merchandise That Doesnt Exist But Should
display_title: EVA Merchandise That Doesnt Exist But Should
wikitext:
[]: EVA Merchandise That Doesn't Exist But Should             Quote Post by WellManicuredMan » Thu Feb 18, 2010 5:34 pm I am well aware that almost anything that Gainax can sell as Evangelion merchandise will be sold as Evangelion merchandise--but that leaves a big "almost." What NGE product hasn't Gainax made yet that you would want to see? Personally, I love collector's edition DVDs and box sets. Whenever I buy anything, I always try to go for the ones with director's commentaries, making-of featurettes, etc. One of my favorites would be the special edition of Terminator 2: Judgment Day , which has enlightening text commentary and very interesting documentaries on how the movie was made. And, of course, the Alien box set, which has nine discs, oodles of special features, and a very cool case, all for under thirty bucks. The Dirty Harry collector's edition also deserves mention for including a shitload of booklets and material to go along with it--something that I also enjoyed in the box sets for Fullmetal Alchemist . My dream piece of EVA paraphernalia would have to be a box set like that, including the whole series (director's cuts included), Death and Rebirth , End of Evangelion , and some special goodies like director's commentary, making-of featurettes, and so on and so forth. Maybe some booklets or some other kind of feelies. And, of course, a nice shiny box to hold it all in.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Feb 18, 2010 7:06 pm Naked Eva Realdolls. (I swear there's been a thread where I had the opportunity to post that before...)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Feb 19, 2010 2:01 am At least one, if my memory serves. Probably after one of my suggestions that Gainax cut the hypocrisy and just add official Asuka and Rei realdolls to their product line.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Fri Feb 19, 2010 2:03 am action figures of some of the supporting cast even more puzzling is the lack of Gendo and Kaji action figures, especially

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Fri Feb 19, 2010 2:08 am I want Games Workshop to adapt the franchise. It would have to be JSSDF, Seele, and Nerv pitted against each other, because one alien versus one EVA kills interest pretty fast on the tabletop.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Fri Feb 19, 2010 2:11 am now, if they did make action figures of some of the supporting cast, I may end up having to buy three Mayas, though only one will remain in its original condition, while the ot...no, i'll need four that's right

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Fri Feb 19, 2010 5:43 am GasmaskAvenger wrote: now, if they did make action figures of some of the supporting cast, I may end up having to buy three Mayas, though only one will remain in its original condition, while the ot...no, i'll need four that's right

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Fri Feb 19, 2010 8:47 am How 'bout a stateside Blu-Rei release of EoE and D&R. I'd be a happy camper.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaRaver » Fri Feb 19, 2010 8:49 am WMM: I think they did produce a DVD box set including everything listed in your original post. The entire TV series, plus director's cut episodes, plus Death and Rebirth and End of Evangelion. They never sold it in the US though. I think it was only released in Japan, and possibly some other Asian countries. I'll see if I can find more info on it for you, but I know it does not have English duds or subs. The GW adaptation is a great idea. I could see that being fun. I think you would need more than just three armies though, and the details would be tricky to work out. I would like to see GNR action figures. I think Rei looks awesome with the wings, but I have only once seen a paper-weight type thing, but it wasn't the whole figure. I was cut off where her legs are below the clouds when she first appears in EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Feb 19, 2010 10:11 am I'm surprised no one has mentioned Evangelion Entry Plug Tampons "to help prevent third impact".

[]:             Quote Post by Natsuka_Chie » Fri Feb 19, 2010 10:17 am Joseph the PRPD wrote: I'm surprised no one has mentioned Evangelion Entry Plug Tampons "to help prevent third impact".

[]:             Quote Post by WellManicuredMan » Fri Feb 19, 2010 12:15 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: I'm surprised no one has mentioned Evangelion Entry Plug Tampons "to help prevent third impact".

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Feb 19, 2010 12:38 pm I for one would like to see a decent Evangelion fighting game. Preferably 2D. Preferably in the vein of Guilty Gear, but with less air-time.

[]:             Quote Post by giuliano » Fri Feb 19, 2010 1:05 pm @WellManicuredMan: They released two really nice limited edition DVD box sets in Japan that I can remember, one with a see-through red outer case containing the entire series plus movies and directors cuts. http://page9.auctions.yahoo.co.jp/jp/auction/k126226465 The other was released in 3 parts and was called 'Second Impact Box' (parts 1, 2 & 3) that also included the entire series+movies and even the Girlfriend of Steel game. http://page.auctions.yahoo.co.jp/jp/auction/133259135 Both I believe didn't have english subs... @Electric Sachiel: I recently found out they've been releasing the Angels under a range called 'Soul of Soft': http://www.hlj.com/hljlist2/?Word=evangelion%20soft&Dis=2&from=detail&searchpage=top_page @EvaRaver: I've got one GNR (quite small Sega released figure) pictured here: I like it quite a lot, but yeah I second a good quality GNR being made

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Fri Feb 19, 2010 1:10 pm Electric Sachiel wrote: LOL. Just what do you intend to do with them if you ever get to possess these Maya figures?

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Fri Feb 19, 2010 1:29 pm An airsoft pallet gun would go nicely with the replica prog knife.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Feb 19, 2010 1:42 pm Wasn't a Maya figure part of one of the Portraits series? The big release of everything Evangelion was Renewal, and it included tons of literature.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Fri Feb 19, 2010 2:35 pm Ornette wrote: Wasn't a Maya figure part of one of the Portraits series?

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Sat Feb 20, 2010 8:05 pm I want to see the manga re-release into one collection.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sat Feb 20, 2010 9:04 pm WellManicuredMan wrote: This is "Evangelion merchandise that doesn't exist but should", not "Evangelion merchandise that doesn't exist and shouldn't."
